steps.title: Identify coefficients
steps.content: Rewrite the equation in standard form $$Ax+By=C$$: here $$10x - 5y = -12$$ gives $A=10$, $B=-5$, and $C=-12$.

steps.title: Compute slope
steps.content: The slope is $$-\frac{A}{B}$$ = $$-\frac{10}{-5}$$ = 2. That matches choice D — D is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice D is correct. A linear equation can be written in the form $y=mx+b$, where $m$ is the slope of the graph of the equation in the xy-plane and $\left(0,b\right)$ is the y-intercept. Subtracting $10x$ from each side of the given equation, $10x-5y=-12$, yields $-5y=-10x-12$. Dividing each side of this equation by $-5$ yields $y=2x+\frac{12}{5}$. This equation is in the form $y=mx+b$, where $m=2$. Therefore, the slope of the graph of the given equation in the xy-plane is $2$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
